name: Blog Post
description: Conversational, opinion-driven blog post for developer and writer audiences

voice.sentenceLength: varied — mix short punchy sentences with occasional longer ones for rhythm
voice.paragraphStyle: short-punchy — most paragraphs 1-3 sentences, single idea per paragraph
voice.rhetoricalStyle: direct-assertion — states claims boldly, earns them with examples not hedging

voiceBehaviors: Favors sentence fragments for emphasis when the rhythm calls for it
Avoids corporate hedging phrases ('it is worth noting', 'it should be mentioned', 'one might argue')
Keeps vocabulary in everyday register — Anglo-Saxon words over Latinate when both work
Uses contractions consistently — 'don't' not 'do not', 'isn't' not 'is not'
Addresses the reader directly with 'you' — assumes a peer relationship
Allows one-sentence paragraphs for emphasis without flagging them as fragments
Rhetorical questions are acceptable sparingly to set up a point, not as filler

structure.introStyle: hook-first — opens with a specific observation, question, or claim that earns the reader's next sentence
structure.endingStyle: strong-close — lands on a resonant final line, not a summary of what was said

criteria.name: novelty
criteria.description: Does it say something worth saying? Not obvious, not generic. The reader should encounter at least one idea they haven't seen phrased this way before.

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.description: Every sentence is unambiguous on first read. No sentence requires re-reading to parse. Technical terms are explained or contextually obvious.

criteria.name: structure
criteria.description: The arc holds. Opening earns its ending. Each section transitions naturally to the next. The piece has a discernible shape, not a list of loosely related points.

criteria.name: voice_preservation
criteria.description: Still sounds like the author, not like a language model. Sentence rhythm, vocabulary choices, and rhetorical habits match the original draft's voice fingerprint.

criteria.name: audience_fit
criteria.description: Right register and assumed knowledge for the target reader. Does not over-explain basics the audience knows. Does not under-explain domain concepts the audience may not know.

criteria.name: concision
criteria.description: No filler sentences. Nothing repeats. Every paragraph advances the piece. If a sentence can be cut without losing meaning, it should be.

criteria.name: factual_integrity
criteria.description: No invented citations, no fabricated claims, no silently altered positions. Every factual claim in the revision must trace to the original draft or be flagged as added.
